name: 8. 고등 부정·특수 비교구문 150
description: 부분부정/완전부정, 부정어 관용(hardly/seldom/no longer), 이중부정(cannot but/never ~ without), not/no more/less than, at most/least/best, as ~ as any/the last ~ to V 150개 (고등 수준)

words.w: He is not always right.
words.m: [부분부정] 그가 항상 맞는 것은 아니다. (not always)

words.w: Not all birds can fly.
words.m: [부분부정] 모든 새가 날 수 있는 것은 아니다. (not all)

words.w: Not every student passed.
words.m: [부분부정] 모든 학생이 통과한 것은 아니다. (not every)

words.w: Both of them are not guilty.
words.m: [부분부정] 둘 다 죄가 있는 것은 아니다. (not both)

words.w: He is not necessarily wrong.
words.m: [부분부정] 그가 반드시 틀린 것은 아니다. (not necessarily)

words.w: I don't always agree.
Not every movie is interesting.
She doesn't always work late.
words.m: [부분부정] 그녀는 항상 늦게까지 일하는 것은 아니다.

words.w: Not all children like vegetables.
words.m: [부분부정] 모든 아이가 채소를 좋아하는 것은 아니다.

words.w: He is not entirely happy.
Not everyone agrees with me.
Not all news is good.
He doesn't know everything.
words.m: [부분부정] 그가 모든 것을 아는 것은 아니다.

words.w: Not every question has an answer.
words.m: [부분부정] 모든 질문에 답이 있는 것은 아니다.

words.w: I don't fully understand.
Not all my friends came.
words.m: [부분부정] 내 친구 모두가 온 것은 아니다.

words.w: She is not completely wrong.
Both answers are not correct.
words.m: [부분부정] 두 답이 다 맞는 것은 아니다.

words.w: Not every effort succeeds.
He is not always honest.
I don't know at all.
Nobody understood.
Nothing is impossible.
None of us agreed.
No one came.
I have never lied.
He has no time at all.
Nothing scares me.
None of them know the truth.
Nobody lives there.
I never forget.
She has no idea.
Not a single person came.
words.m: [완전부정] 단 한 명도 오지 않았다. (not a single)

words.w: There is no way.
Nothing can stop me.
I can hardly believe it.
words.m: [부정어 관용] 거의 믿을 수 없다. (hardly = 거의 ~하지 않다)

words.w: She scarcely spoke.
He seldom visits us.
words.m: [부정어 관용] 그는 우리를 거의 방문하지 않는다. (seldom)

words.w: We rarely eat out.
words.m: [부정어 관용] 우리는 외식을 거의 하지 않는다. (rarely)

words.w: I barely finished.
He is no longer a student.
words.m: [부정어 관용] 그는 더 이상 학생이 아니다. (no longer)

words.w: She can hardly wait.
words.m: [부정어 관용] 그녀는 거의 기다릴 수 없다.

words.w: I hardly know him.
Seldom does he complain.
Rarely have I met such kindness.
He barely made it on time.
She no longer works here.
words.m: [부정어 관용] 그녀는 더 이상 여기서 일하지 않는다.

words.w: I hardly ever watch TV.
words.m: [부정어 관용] 나는 TV를 거의 안 본다.

words.w: We scarcely had time to eat.
words.m: [부정어 관용] 우리는 먹을 시간이 거의 없었다.

words.w: He seldom smiles.
I barely know her.
She rarely eats meat.
Hardly anyone came.
I can scarcely hear you.
words.m: [부정어 관용] 네 말이 거의 들리지 않는다.

words.w: He rarely makes mistakes.
No longer do I feel sad.
words.m: [부정어 관용] 나는 더 이상 슬프지 않다. (도치)

words.w: Seldom have I seen such beauty.
She hardly slept.
I barely passed the test.
He rarely travels.
He cannot help laughing.
words.m: [이중부정] 그는 웃지 않을 수 없다. (cannot help ~ing)

words.w: I can't but agree.
words.m: [이중부정] 나는 동의하지 않을 수 없다. (cannot but + 원형)

words.w: She never fails to call.
There is no one who doesn't love her.
You cannot be too careful.
I cannot help crying.
He never sees her without thinking of his mother.
words.m: [이중부정] 그는 그녀를 볼 때마다 어머니를 생각한다. (never ~ without)

words.w: No parent doesn't love their child.
I never meet him without smiling.
You can't overestimate his kindness.
words.m: [이중부정] 그의 친절은 아무리 높이 평가해도 지나치지 않다.

words.w: She cannot but smile.
He never reads without learning something.
No one can deny that he is talented.
words.m: [이중부정] 그가 재능 있다는 것을 부인할 수 없다.

words.w: I cannot help but agree.
words.m: [이중부정] 동의하지 않을 수 없다. (cannot help but + 원형)

words.w: Nothing is impossible; there is no limit.
I have not more than $10.
words.m: [not/no more than] 나는 기껏해야 10달러 있다. (not more than = at most)

words.w: She has no more than $5.
words.m: [not/no more than] 그녀는 5달러밖에 없다. (no more than = only)

words.w: He is not more than 40 years old.
I had no more than 3 hours of sleep.
words.m: [not/no more than] 나는 겨우 3시간밖에 못 잤다.

words.w: There are not more than 20 people.
She ate no more than one apple.
words.m: [not/no more than] 그녀는 사과 하나밖에 안 먹었다.

words.w: I wait not more than 10 minutes.
He earned no more than $100.
The book has not more than 200 pages.
She spoke no more than 5 words.
I walked not more than 5 km.
He has read no more than 10 books.
words.m: [not/no more than] 그는 겨우 10권밖에 못 읽었다.

words.w: It took not more than an hour.
words.m: [not/no more than] 기껏해야 한 시간 걸렸다.

words.w: She slept no more than 4 hours.
words.m: [not/no more than] 그녀는 겨우 4시간밖에 못 잤다.

words.w: There are not more than 50 members.
He wrote no more than 3 emails.
words.m: [not/no more than] 그는 이메일을 겨우 3통 썼다.

words.w: I have not more than 5 friends here.
She weighs no more than 50 kg.
The meeting was not more than an hour.
words.m: [not/no more than] 회의는 기껏해야 한 시간이었다.

words.w: He talks no more than necessary.
words.m: [not/no more than] 그는 필요 이상 말하지 않는다.

words.w: There are not less than 100 people.
words.m: [not/no less than] 적어도 100명이 있다. (not less than = at least)

words.w: He has no less than $1,000.
words.m: [not/no less than] 그는 1,000달러나 가지고 있다. (no less than = as much as)

words.w: The book has not less than 500 pages.
She read no less than 20 books.
I waited not less than an hour.
words.m: [not/no less than] 나는 적어도 한 시간 기다렸다.

words.w: He worked no less than 12 hours.
She donated not less than $500.
There were no less than 1000 attendees.
He jogs not less than 5 km every day.
words.m: [not/no less than] 그는 매일 적어도 5km 조깅한다.

words.w: She wrote no less than 10 novels.
words.m: [not/no less than] 그녀는 10편이나 되는 소설을 썼다.

words.w: The journey takes not less than 3 hours.
words.m: [not/no less than] 그 여정은 적어도 3시간 걸린다.

words.w: He earned no less than $500,000.
The building is not less than 50 years old.
words.m: [not/no less than] 그 건물은 적어도 50년 됐다.

words.w: She weighs no less than 80 kg.
There are not less than 10 students.
He has no less than 5 cars.
words.m: [not/no less than] 그는 5대나 되는 차를 갖고 있다.

words.w: I slept not less than 8 hours.
She has visited no less than 30 countries.
The event lasted not less than 3 days.
He read no less than 100 pages.
I have $10 at most.
The trip takes 3 hours at most.
I study 2 hours at least every day.
words.m: [at most/least/best] 나는 매일 적어도 2시간 공부한다. (at least)

words.w: She is 30 at least.
The cake serves 10 people at most.
He has 100 books at least.
At the best, we can finish by Friday.
words.m: [at most/least/best] 잘해 봐야 금요일까지 끝낼 수 있다. (at the best)

words.w: She weighs 50 kg at most.
I've read this book 5 times at least.
This job pays $20 an hour at best.
words.m: [at most/least/best] 이 일은 잘해 봐야 시간당 20달러다.

words.w: We have 20 minutes at most.
He is 25 at least.
The car costs $5,000 at most.
I need 3 days at least.
At best, he's a beginner.
He is as kind as any man I know.
words.m: [as~as any / the last~to V] 그는 내가 아는 어떤 사람만큼 친절하다. (as ~ as any = 최상급 대용)

words.w: She is as smart as any student in her class.
words.m: [as~as any / the last~to V] 그녀는 반의 어떤 학생만큼 똑똑하다.

words.w: This book is as good as any novel.
words.m: [as~as any / the last~to V] 이 책은 어떤 소설만큼 좋다.

words.w: He runs as fast as any athlete.
words.m: [as~as any / the last~to V] 그는 어떤 선수만큼 빨리 달린다.

words.w: She cooks as well as any chef.
words.m: [as~as any / the last~to V] 그녀는 어떤 요리사만큼 요리를 잘한다.

words.w: He is the last person to lie.
words.m: [as~as any / the last~to V] 그는 거짓말할 사람이 결코 아니다. (the last ~ to V)

words.w: She is the last one to give up.
words.m: [as~as any / the last~to V] 그녀는 결코 포기할 사람이 아니다.

words.w: I am the last to complain.
words.m: [as~as any / the last~to V] 나는 결코 불평할 사람이 아니다.

words.w: He is the last man to steal.
words.m: [as~as any / the last~to V] 그는 결코 훔칠 사람이 아니다.

words.w: She is the last person to betray a friend.
words.m: [as~as any / the last~to V] 그녀는 결코 친구를 배신할 사람이 아니다.

words.w: This is as beautiful as any view.
words.m: [as~as any / the last~to V] 이것은 어떤 경치만큼 아름답다.

words.w: He is as brave as any soldier.
words.m: [as~as any / the last~to V] 그는 어떤 군인만큼 용감하다.

words.w: She is as patient as any teacher.
words.m: [as~as any / the last~to V] 그녀는 어떤 선생님만큼 참을성 있다.

words.w: This dish is as tasty as any I've had.
words.m: [as~as any / the last~to V] 이 음식은 내가 먹어본 어떤 것만큼 맛있다.

words.w: She is as talented as any singer.
words.m: [as~as any / the last~to V] 그녀는 어떤 가수만큼 재능 있다.

words.w: He is the last person to tell a secret.
words.m: [as~as any / the last~to V] 그는 결코 비밀을 말할 사람이 아니다.

words.w: She's the last one to arrive late.
words.m: [as~as any / the last~to V] 그녀는 결코 늦게 도착할 사람이 아니다.

words.w: I am the last to cry in public.
words.m: [as~as any / the last~to V] 나는 결코 사람들 앞에서 울 사람이 아니다.

words.w: He's the last man to refuse help.
words.m: [as~as any / the last~to V] 그는 결코 도움을 거절할 사람이 아니다.

words.w: This is as strong as any product.
words.m: [as~as any / the last~to V] 이것은 어떤 제품만큼 튼튼하다.
